message:
perf(frankenterm-core/storage): two-stage FTS search + selective startup-sync

Two related performance improvements to the SQLite FTS5 path in
crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs.

1. Two-stage rank + hydrate for `search_fts_with_snippets` (ft-okhhj)

The previous implementation issued a single FTS query that ran
`snippet(...)` and `highlight(...)` over every matching row before the
BM25 sort + LIMIT was applied. On panes with thousands of matching
segments this materialized highlighted content for many rows that never
crossed the LIMIT cutoff, and dominated query cost.

The new path splits this into:
  - Rank stage: `(rowid, score)` plus tie-break columns only, ordered
    by BM25 with `LIMIT ?` applied. No content / snippet / highlight.
  - Hydration stage: re-queries `output_segments` and the FTS table
    for just the top-N rowids and computes `snippet()`/`highlight()`
    only for them.

A new `SearchOptions::include_highlights: Option<bool>` field lets
callers opt out of `highlight()` while still receiving snippets — useful
when the UI only renders the snippet column. It defaults to whatever
`include_snippets` is, preserving the historical behavior. When
`include_snippets=false`, search falls back to the existing rank-only
single-stage path (`search_fts_rank_only`), which never ran snippet/
highlight anyway.

Concurrency note: a row can only disappear between the rank and
hydration queries if it's deleted concurrently; in that case the row is
dropped silently rather than failing the whole search.

A `tracing::trace!` line records `rank_count` / `hydrate_count` for
each two-stage call so operators can see the cutoff working.

2. Selective `sync_fts_on_startup` pane list

`sync_fts_on_startup` previously fetched every distinct `pane_id` in
`output_segments` and walked them all, even when most panes had nothing
to do. It now joins against `fts_pane_progress` and returns only panes
where `MAX(s.seq) > MAX(p.last_indexed_seq)` (or where there is no
progress row at all). The new helper is `panes_needing_fts_sync` and
replaces the inline pane-list query.

Also includes a small rustfmt touch-up on the SCHEMA_SQL pragma split
and the `READ_POOL` static type annotation.

Behavior is unchanged for callers that don't set the new option.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
